title: carex neopolycephala var. neopolycephala
intro: Carex neopolycephala var. neopolycephala, also known as the Japanese sedge, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Cyperaceae, characterized by its unique, rounded inflorescence. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Carex neopolycephala var. neopolycephala include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
